updateMetadata.rationale: persist risk and flow decisions for prompt reuse
updateMetadata.expectedGain: lower prompt fatigue and stronger auditability
updateMetadata.potentialImpact: decision expiry and context-hash logic becomes mandatory

decisions.context: D10 lifecycle commands execution model
decisions.decision: skills-only: /commit, /pr, /acho are markdown skills the AI reads, not Python CLI commands. cli_commands/workflow.py removed from plan.

decisions.context: Phase 2 vs Phase 5 quality standards direction
decisions.decision: standards-first: Phase 2 defines the quality contract (thresholds, metrics, gate structure). Phase 5 skills implement it. No circular dependency or back-alignment pass.

decisions.context: Task 1.7 vs Phase 7 scope collision on root files
decisions.decision: Task 1.7 scoped to deletion-reference cleanup only. Phase 7 (Tasks 7.4, 7.6, 7.7) does the comprehensive rewrite of copilot-instructions.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md.

decisions.context: Orphan .prompt.md files in context/product/
decisions.decision: Delete both: plan-aiEngineeringRewrite.prompt.md and plan-context-architecture.prompt.md. Content fully absorbed by specs/001-rewrite-v2/. Added as Task 1.4b.

decisions.context: Skill and agent document consistency
decisions.decision: Mandatory templates defined in plan.md § Document Templates. All skills follow: Purpose → Trigger → Procedure → Output Contract → Governance Notes → References. All agents follow: Identity → Capabilities → Activation → Behavior → Referenced Skills → Referenced Standards → Output Contract → Boundaries.

decisions.context: Phase 0 completion status
decisions.decision: Phase 0 (Tasks 0.1-0.3) marked as complete. Spec files and _active.md already exist. Branch creation (0.1) to be executed when S1 starts.

decisions.context: Pre-commit gates (gitleaks, ruff) unavailable — tools not installed/accessible in corporate environment
decisions.decision: Risk accepted: bypass pre-commit and pre-push gates for Markdown-only changes (product-contract.md, learnings.md). No code changes, no secrets risk. IT Security ticket opened for tool access.
decisions.remediation: All three tools now installed and operational: gitleaks 8.30.0, ruff 0.15.0, semgrep 1.151.0. Pre-commit and pre-push hooks active and hash-verified. Gate bypass no longer needed.

decisions.context: Evolving S0-001 — skills need visibility as Claude Code slash commands
decisions.decision: Thin command wrappers in .claude/commands/ point to canonical skill/agent files. No content duplication — each wrapper is a 3-5 line prompt that reads and executes the canonical file. Skills remain the source of truth; command files are pointers only.

decisions.context: Claude Code project-level optimization — settings, session protocol, installer, command enrichment
decisions.decision: Project settings (.claude/settings.json) provide pre-approved permissions for governed tooling. Session Start Protocol added to CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md. Installer deploys full .claude/ tree. Command wrappers enriched with preconditions. No Claude hooks — git hooks enforce agnostically.

decisions.context: Skills taxonomy reorganization — category naming and structure
decisions.decision: Reorganize skills from 6 categories (33 skills) to 7 categories (32 skills). Eliminate tautological swe/ by splitting into dev/ (6), review/ (3), docs/ (4). Rename lifecycle/ to govern/ (9). Absorb validation/ into quality/ (3). Merge pr-creation.md into workflows/pr.md (net -1 skill). Rename files for brevity: dependency-update→deps-update, architecture-analysis→architecture, python-mastery→python-patterns, etc. Category names are activity-based, short (3-8 chars), lowercase, no hyphens.

decisions.context: Multi-model routing scope in spec-021
decisions.decision: Remove multi-model routing from implementation scope. Current platform tooling does not provide robust cross-model runtime orchestration in this repo flow; single-platform execution is deterministic and reliable today.

decisions.context: Agent count and architecture strategy
decisions.decision: Keep agent count small and perspective-based. Avoid 20+ near-duplicate role agents; preserve maintainability and stronger governance consistency.

decisions.context: Strategic gap agent choice — navigator vs delivery-lead/product-manager
decisions.decision: Add navigator instead of delivery-lead/product-manager agents. Navigator fits existing artifacts and strategic gap without requiring external process systems (Jira/sprints/people management).

decisions.context: Data domain capability architecture
decisions.decision: Implement data capability as skills (dev/data-modeling, review/data-security) plus references (database-patterns.md), not as a separate agent. Data work requires procedures and references, not a separate judgment persona.

decisions.context: AI PR review default posture in generated CI/CD
decisions.decision: AI PR review is mandatory in generated CI/CD with high/critical merge blocking. Enforces consistent security and quality posture by default.

decisions.context: VCS CLI tooling unavailability handling
decisions.decision: API-first fallback is required when CLI tooling/policies are blocked. Prevents incomplete installations in restricted enterprise environments.

decisions.context: Patterns skill category purpose and placement
decisions.decision: Remove patterns/ skill category and migrate to references/. Pattern catalogs are reference knowledge, not executable procedures.

decisions.context: Coverage threshold conflict across audit-code/SKILL.md, product-contract.md, and quality/core.md
decisions.decision: 90% is the authoritative overall coverage target (quality/core.md is source of truth). 100% applies only to governance-critical paths. All files aligned to this baseline.

decisions.context: Database-engineer agent vs data-modeling skill-only approach (extends D021-004)
decisions.decision: Add database-engineer agent alongside existing data-modeling skill. Complex DB work requires judgment persona (schema trade-offs, perf tuning, migration risk). Skill provides procedure; agent provides judgment. D021-004 is extended, not contradicted.

decisions.context: Cross-cutting standards (azure, infrastructure, database) placement
decisions.decision: Place in standards/framework/stacks/ alongside primary stack standards. Same path pattern. Marked as cross-cutting (no enforcement gates, advisory only). Applied alongside a primary stack, not instead of one.

decisions.context: TypeScript as base standard for framework-specific stacks
decisions.decision: typescript.md serves as base for React, React Native, NestJS, Astro, Next.js stacks. Framework-specific stacks extend TS base to avoid content duplication. Each framework adds only framework-specific patterns.

decisions.context: OS-native secret storage library choice for cross-platform credential management
decisions.decision: Use `keyring` library for OS-native secret storage. Provides cross-OS abstraction over Windows Credential Manager, macOS Keychain, and Linux libsecret. Pure Python, well-maintained, no native compilation needed.

decisions.context: Sonar gate behavior when SONAR_TOKEN is not configured
decisions.decision: Sonar gate is optional and uses silent-skip. Framework principle: mandatory gates cannot be weakened, but optional integrations should not block teams that don't use them.

decisions.context: Platform onboarding timing relative to install flow
decisions.decision: Platform onboarding is opt-in during install and re-runnable via `ai-eng setup`. Framework contract §6: install must ensure first-commit readiness. Platform auth is helpful but not required for local governance.

decisions.context: CLI subcommand architecture for platform onboarding
decisions.decision: New `setup` CLI subcommand group instead of extending `install`. `install` has a defined contract (copy templates, hooks, readiness). Platform onboarding is a separate concern with its own re-runnability requirement.

decisions.context: Location and format for platform configuration metadata
decisions.decision: `tools.json` in `.ai-engineering/state/` for platform metadata. Follows existing state file pattern. Only non-secret metadata stored. File is system-managed (ownership model).

decisions.context: Sonar gate as separate skill vs embedded in audit-code
decisions.decision: New skill `dev/sonar-gate` rather than embedding in `audit-code`. Skills are single-responsibility. `audit-code` orchestrates gates; `sonar-gate` provides the Sonar-specific procedure. Follows existing pattern.

decisions.context: SonarLint IDE configuration mode: Connected Mode vs standalone rules
decisions.decision: Use Connected Mode pointing to the configured SonarCloud/SonarQube server. Connected Mode syncs rules from the server, ensuring parity between IDE diagnostics and CI quality gate. Standalone mode would require maintaining separate rule sets.

decisions.context: VS Code fork IDEs config path strategy
decisions.decision: VS Code family IDEs (Cursor, Windsurf, Antigravity) all read settings from `.vscode/settings.json` and `.vscode/extensions.json`. One configuration serves all forks. No need for separate `.cursor/`, `.windsurf/`, or `.antigravity/` paths.

decisions.context: Strategy for merging SonarLint settings into existing IDE config files
decisions.decision: Read existing JSON/XML, deep-merge only SonarLint-namespaced keys, write back. Never overwrite user-configured keys outside the `sonarlint` namespace. Creates config directories if missing.

decisions.context: Documentation gate enforcement in commit/PR/acho workflows for OSS GitHub users
decisions.decision: Add mandatory documentation gate to /commit, /pr, and /acho workflows. Classifies changes as user-visible vs internal-only. User-visible changes always update CHANGELOG.md and README.md targeting OSS GitHub users. External documentation portal support: asks for repo URL, clones, updates, creates PR with auto-complete. Internal-only changes skip silently. Uses changelog and writer skills as referenced procedures.

decisions.context: Deprecate /pre-implementation skill — duplicates /cleanup + /create-spec
decisions.decision: Absorb /pre-implementation into /cleanup (enhanced with repo status + spec reset) and /create-spec (composes /cleanup). /pre-implementation deleted. /cleanup becomes the single hygiene primitive. Session Start Protocol updated to reference /cleanup. Skill count reduced by 1 (workflows: 7→6).

decisions.context: .NET and Azure standards expansion strategy
decisions.decision: Expand existing standards (dotnet.md, azure.md) as single source of truth for all .NET knowledge. Zero new skills — existing generic skills (api-design, test-runner, database-ops, cicd-generate, infrastructure, performance) already read applicable stack standards and will automatically consume expanded patterns. Principal-engineer agent evolved from read-only reviewer to read-write implementor with multi-stack references.

decisions.context: Selective test execution rollout and safety envelope
decisions.decision: Adopt module-scoped selective test execution with a single source of truth (`policy/test_scope.py`) shared by pre-push and CI. Rollout mode starts in shadow (`AI_ENG_TEST_SCOPE_MODE=shadow`), then switches to enforce by configuration. Safety nets are fail-closed: unknown/unmapped source changes, src deletes, high-risk/config triggers, base-ref/merge-base failures, and scope computation errors force full tier execution. Emergency bypass remains `AI_ENG_TEST_SCOPE=off`. Rollback criteria: revert scoped gate/CI wiring if mapping integrity fails more than 3 times per week or any scoped miss is detected; temporarily restore full-suite execution until remediated.
